document_title: LoRa Base Station and Car Radio Evaluation Board Hardware Verification Procedure
scope_text: This document provides procedures to verify the primary hardware requirements for the LoRa Base Station and Car Radio Evaluation Boards as specified in the Hardware Specifications document AE104193-001. This procedure is intended to be performed as part of the initial design verification testing.

ref_docs.title: LoRa Base Station & Car Radio Evaluation Board Hardware Specifications
LoRa Radio Hub & Traincar Evaluation Board Software Specifications

test_exec_text: This test procedure shall be executed by qualified personnel. All results shall be recorded in the associated datasheet. Any deviation from the procedure must be documented and approved by the test lead. The unit under test (UUT) shall be either the LoRa Base Station or the LoRa Car Radio, as specified in each step.
ds_reporting_text: Any test step that results in a 'Fail' shall be considered a discrepancy. The test operator must stop, document the failure with detailed observations (including equipment used, settings, and measured values), and report it to the test lead. A Discrepancy Report (DR) shall be generated for each failure before proceeding, unless otherwise instructed.

equipment_table.desc: CAT II or higher, at least 4.5 digits
Windows or Linux, with serial terminal software (e.g., Tera Term, PuTTY)

procedure_rt_markdown:
1.0 PRE-TEST SETUP (font-size: 12, bold: yes)
1.1 Visually inspect the LoRa Base Station Evaluation Board (BS) and LoRa Car Radio Evaluation Board (CR) for any obvious damage. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
1.2 Connect the Host PC to the debug USB port on the unit under test (UUT). (font-size: 11, bold: no)
1.3 Open a serial terminal application on the Host PC and configure it for the UUT's COM port. (font-size: 11, bold: no)

2.0 POWER SUBSYSTEM VERIFICATION (font-size: 12, bold: yes)
2.1 Set the DC Power Supply to +5VDC with a 2A current limit. Do not enable the output yet. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
2.2 Connect the barrel jack power cable from the DC Power Supply to the power input on the UUT (start with BS). (font-size: 11, bold: no)
2.3 Enable the DC Power Supply output. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
2.4 Verify that the UUT powers on. Check for status LEDs and boot messages in the serial terminal. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
2.5 From the serial terminal debug interface, issue the command to read the primary power input draw. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
2.6 Verify the reported power draw is within 10% of the value displayed on the DC Power Supply. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
2.7 Power down the BS and repeat steps 2.2 through 2.6 for the CR. (font-size: 11, bold: no)

3.0 LOCAL PROCESSOR AND MEMORY VERIFICATION (font-size: 12, bold: yes)
3.1 Base Station (BS) (font-size: 11, bold: yes)
3.1.1 With the BS powered on and connected to the serial terminal, issue the command to display system information. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
3.1.2 Verify the processor is identified as ATSAMA5D27-D1G. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
3.1.3 Verify the system information reports at least 1GByte of external Flash memory. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
3.2 Car Radio (CR) (font-size: 11, bold: yes)
3.2.1 With the CR powered on and connected to the serial terminal, issue the command to display system information. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
3.2.2 Verify the processor is identified as a member of the STM32L4 series. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
3.2.3 Verify the system information reports at least 64MBytes of external Flash memory. (font-size: 11, bold: no)

4.0 LORA RADIO SUBSYSTEM VERIFICATION (font-size: 12, bold: yes)
4.1 Visually inspect the BS and CR boards. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
4.2 Verify the LoRa radio chipset is marked SX1276. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
4.3 Verify the RF input for the LoRa subsystem is an SMA connector on both boards. (font-size: 11, bold: no)

5.0 ETHERNET SUBSYSTEM VERIFICATION (BASE STATION ONLY) (font-size: 12, bold: yes)
5.1 Power on the BS. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
5.2 Visually inspect the BS board and verify the presence of a standard RJ45 Ethernet port. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
5.3 Connect an Ethernet cable from the BS RJ45 port to a network switch that supports 10/100Mbps. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
5.4 Verify the Ethernet link and activity LEDs on the RJ45 port illuminate, indicating a successful link. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
5.5 From the serial terminal, issue a command to check the network interface status. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
5.6 Verify the link speed is reported as 10Mbps or 100Mbps. (font-size: 11, bold: no)

6.0 USB DEBUG PORT VERIFICATION (font-size: 12, bold: yes)
6.1 Using the connection established in step 1.3, verify that serial communication is functional by typing a 'help' or similar command and observing the expected response for both UUTs. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
6.2 Place the UUT into programming mode according to its specific instructions. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
6.3 Using the appropriate software tool on the Host PC, attempt to connect to the processor's programming interface via the USB connection. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
6.4 Verify the connection is successful. (A mock programming operation, like reading the device ID, is sufficient). (font-size: 11, bold: no)
6.5 Repeat for the other UUT. (font-size: 11, bold: no)

7.0 EXTERNAL IO VERIFICATION (font-size: 12, bold: yes)
7.1 Visually inspect both the BS and CR boards. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.2 Verify a 2-row, 100mil header is present for external IO. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.3 Using a DMM, check the pinout of the header on a UUT. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.4 Verify one row consists entirely of GND pins. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.5 Verify two pins measure +3.3V. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.6 From the serial terminal, issue commands to toggle each of the 8 GPIO signal pins high and low. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.7 Using an oscilloscope or DMM, verify each GPIO pin toggles between ~0V and ~3.3V accordingly. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.8 From the serial terminal, issue commands to set the 2 DAC signals to 0V, 1.65V, and 3.3V. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.9 Using a DMM, verify the DAC output voltages are within 5% of the set values. (font-size: 11, bold: no)
7.10 This concludes the hardware verification procedure. (font-size: 11, bold: no)

datasheet_rows.expected: No damage found on BS or CR boards.
UUT powers on, status LEDs illuminate, boot messages appear in terminal.
Reported power draw is within 10% of supply value.
Reported power draw is within 10% of supply value.
Processor identified as ATSAMA5D27-D1G.
System reports >= 1GByte external Flash.
Processor identified as STM32L4 series.
System reports >= 64MBytes external Flash.
Chipset marking is 'SX1276' on both boards.
An SMA connector is present for the LoRa RF input on both boards.
Standard RJ45 port is present.
Link and activity LEDs illuminate.
Link speed reported as 10/100Mbps.
Serial terminal commands receive expected responses.
Host PC programming tool successfully connects to the processor.
A 2-row, 100mil header is present on both boards.
One full row measures continuity to GND.
Two pins measure +3.3V +/- 5%.
All 8 GPIO pins toggle between ~0V and ~3.3V.
Both DAC pins output voltages within 5% of set values (0V, 1.65V, 3.3V).
